description: YouTube transcript and video downloader (yt-dlp), channel management (Data API v3), and browser-session community posting

auth.usage_instructions: Manage YouTube OAuth authentication for channel-management commands. Use `auth login` to enter Client ID/Secret and complete the browser consent flow, `auth status` to inspect, `auth test` to verify the token can read your channel, and `auth profiles` to manage multiple accounts.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure OAuth credentials and run the consent flow, or pass --credential-type browser_session to save a signed-in YouTube browser session for channel post commands. Community posts require both credential types on the same profile. OAuth prompts for CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET, then opens a browser to grant the youtube + youtube.upload + youtube.force-ssl scopes. Use --profile/-p for a named profile. Use --force/-F to clear the selected credential state and re-authenticate.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored OAuth token and credentials for the active or named profile. Use --profile/-p to target a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage profile files under `youtube auth profiles`. Use list to inspect profiles, create to add one, set-default to switch active profile, and delete to remove one.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile. NAME is required (positional, e.g., 'staging' or 'production').

options.name: --auth-type
options.help: Authentication type for this profile. Valid values: google_oauth_desktop

options.name: --auth-param
options.help: Auth parameter as FIELD=VALUE. Repeat for additional required values

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile. NAME is required (positional). Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a named profile. NAME is required (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show their auth types and active state.
list.usage_instructions: List all configured auth profiles. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Supports --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.
remove.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and its data. NAME is required (positional). Use --force/-F to skip confirmation. Alias of `auth profiles delete`.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.
select.usage_instructions: Activate a profile within its auth type. NAME is required (positional). The selected profile becomes the one used by commands that don't pass --profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Show OAuth credential and token status across profiles. Use --profile/-p to check one profile and --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify the stored OAuth token works by calling channels.list(mine=True). Returns the authenticated channel ID/title on success. Use --table/-t for formatted output, --verbose/-v for detailed checks, and --profile/-p to target one profile.

channel.usage_instructions: Manage the authenticated user's own YouTube channel. `channel videos` uses the YouTube Data API v3 and requires `youtube auth login`. `channel posts` derives the owned channel from the same profile's OAuth identity, then uses the authenticated YouTube web UI and requires both `youtube auth login` and `youtube auth login --credential-type browser_session`.

posts.help: Post messages to a YouTube channel
posts.usage_instructions: Post text messages to the selected profile's owned YouTube channel using the authenticated browser session plus the same profile's OAuth identity. This path is required because Google's current YouTube Data API no longer supports channel bulletin/community-post creation.

create.help: Create a text community post for the authenticated profile's owned channel.
create.usage_instructions: Create a text community post for the selected profile's owned channel. The command derives the channel URL from the same profile's OAuth identity, so the same profile must already have both `youtube auth login` and `youtube auth login --credential-type browser_session`. --message/-m is required. Use --dry-run to validate the composer without publishing. The command fails clearly if YouTube does not show the channel post composer.

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Fill the composer but do not click Post

videos.help: Manage videos on the authenticated user's channel
videos.usage_instructions: Manage videos owned by the authenticated user. Use `list` to enumerate uploads, `get` for full metadata, `upload` to push a local file, `update` to change title/description/tags/privacy/thumbnail, and `delete` to remove a video.

delete.help: Delete a video by ID. Requires confirmation unless --yes is passed.
delete.usage_instructions: Permanently delete a video. VIDEO_ID is required (positional). Prompts for confirmation unless --yes/-y is passed. Use --profile to target a non-default profile.

get.help: Get full metadata for a single video by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Fetch full flattened metadata for one video by ID, including top-level fields like title, description, status, statistics, and duration. VIDEO_ID is required (positional). Use --table/-t for formatted output. When parsing JSON output, read description from top-level `description`, not `snippet.description`.

list.help: List videos on the authenticated user's channel (uploads playlist).
list.usage_instructions: List videos on the authenticated user's channel by paging through the uploads playlist. Use --limit/-l to cap results (default 50). Use --filter/-f for field:op:value filters (e.g., privacy_status:eq:public, title:contains:vlog). Use --properties/-p to narrow output fields and --table/-t for a formatted table. Use --profile to target a non-default profile.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of videos to list

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to include in output

update.help: Update title, description, tags, privacy, or thumbnail of an existing video.
update.usage_instructions: Update an existing video. VIDEO_ID is required (positional). Provide one or more of --title, --description, --tag (repeatable; replaces existing tags), --category-id, --privacy (private|unlisted|public), or --thumbnail (jpg/png path). At least one field must be supplied. Use --profile to target a non-default profile.

options.name: --include-recommended-chapters
options.help: Return AI instructions to add recommended Video Chapters before updating

upload.help: Upload a video file to the authenticated user's channel.
upload.usage_instructions: Upload a local video file to the authenticated user's channel. FILE is required (positional path). --title is required. Use --description, --tag (repeatable), --category-id (default 22 = People & Blogs), --privacy (private|unlisted|public, default private), --publish-at (RFC 3339 timestamp; requires --privacy private to schedule), --made-for-kids/--not-made-for-kids, and --thumbnail (jpg/png path) to set a custom thumbnail after upload. Use --profile to target a non-default profile.

arguments.name: file
arguments.help: Path to video file to upload

options.name: --privacy
options.help: [private|unlisted|public]  Privacy status: private, unlisted, or public

options.name: --publish-at
options.help: Scheduled publish time in RFC 3339 (e.g., 2026-06-01T15:00:00Z). Requires --privacy private

options.name: --made-for-kids
options.help: Mark video as made for kids

options.name: --thumbnail
options.help: Path to thumbnail image (jpg/png) to set after upload

options.name: --include-recommended-chapters
options.help: Return AI instructions to add recommended Video Chapters before uploading

channels.help: Inspect owned channels and channel-creation guidance
channels.usage_instructions: Inspect channels you own across every authenticated profile and get channel-creation guidance. Use `channels list` to see every channel you manage, `channels get` to fetch one by ID, `channels update` to set a banner, and `channels create` for guidance on the unsupported create API.

create.help: Explain how to create a YouTube channel when the API does not support it.
create.usage_instructions: Explain why channel creation is not supported by the YouTube Data API v3 and point to the YouTube web UI. Always exits non-zero; takes no real action.

get.help: Get one owned YouTube channel by channel ID (searches every profile).
get.usage_instructions: Fetch one owned channel by channel ID, searching across every authenticated profile (or just --profile when given). CHANNEL_ID is required (positional). The result is annotated with the source `profile`. Use --table/-t and --properties/-p to shape output.

list.help: List owned YouTube channels across every authenticated profile. Each OAuth token is bound to one channel, so aggregating across profiles is the only way to see every channel you manage. Pass --profile to restrict to one profile's channel.
list.usage_instructions: List owned YouTube channels aggregated across EVERY authenticated profile. Each OAuth token is bound to exactly one channel, so this is the only way to see every channel you manage (e.g., personal plus brand channels). Each row is annotated with the `profile` it came from, and channels reachable from multiple profiles are deduped by channel id. Pass --profile to restrict to a single profile's channel. Use --limit/-l to cap the merged total, --filter/-f and --properties/-p to narrow output, and --table/-t for a formatted table. If any profile's token is invalid or expired, that profile is reported on stderr and the command exits non-zero while still returning the channels that succeeded.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Limit to a single profile's channel. Omit to aggregate every authenticated profile

update.usage_instructions: Update one owned channel's banner image. CHANNEL_ID is required (positional) and --banner-image is required (PNG/JPEG, minimum 2048x1152; oversized images are normalized). Searches across profiles unless --profile is given. Use --table/-t and --properties/-p to shape output.

options.name: --banner-image
options.help: Path to banner image file (PNG/JPEG, minimum 2048x1152)

transcripts.usage_instructions: Download transcripts/subtitles from YouTube videos. Use `transcripts download` with one or more video URLs.

download.help: Download transcripts for one or more YouTube videos.
download.usage_instructions: Download transcripts for YouTube videos. URLS is required (one or more YouTube video URLs, positional). Use --output-dir/-o to set output directory (default: current dir). Use --format/-f for subtitle format (srt, vtt, txt; default: srt). Use --lang/-l for language (default: en). Auto-generated subtitles are included by default; use --no-auto-sub to exclude them, or --manual-sub to prefer manual subtitles. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

videos.usage_instructions: Download actual video files from YouTube. Use `videos download` with URLs or --channel/-c to download all videos from a channel.

validate.help: Validate YouTube manual Video Chapters in a description.

options.name: --duration-seconds
options.help: Video duration in seconds; enables final-chapter length validation

download.help: Download one or more YouTube videos, or all videos from a channel.
download.usage_instructions: Download YouTube videos. Provide one or more video URLs as positional args, OR use --channel/-c with a channel handle (e.g., 'myclassroom') or full URL to download all videos from that channel. Use --output-dir/-o to set output directory (default: current dir). Use --format/-f for container format (mp4, mkv, webm; default: mp4). Use --quality/-q for quality selection (best, worst, or yt-dlp format code; default: best). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --channel
options.help: Download all videos from a channel (handle or URL)

options.name: --folder-path
options.help: Folder to compare and sync into when using --sync

options.name: --quality
options.help: Quality selection (best, worst, or yt-dlp format code)

options.name: --sync
options.help: With --channel, download only recent videos missing from --folder-path

options.name: --limit
options.help: Max videos to download (0 = all, applies to --channel)

get.help: Get metadata for a single YouTube video.
get.usage_instructions: Get metadata for one YouTube video URL. Use --table/-t for formatted output when you need a compact human-readable view.

list.help: List all videos from a YouTube channel.
list.usage_instructions: List videos from a channel without downloading them. CHANNEL is required and can be a handle or URL. Use --limit/-l to cap results, --filter/-f and --properties/-p to narrow output, --table/-t for formatted output, and --exclude-shorts to omit Shorts.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

usage_instructions: Use for downloading YouTube videos and transcripts (yt-dlp public commands), managing the authenticated user's own channel via the YouTube Data API v3 (channel videos list/get/upload/update/delete), and creating text community posts through an authenticated YouTube browser session (`channel posts create`). Public download commands need no auth; video/channel API management requires `youtube auth login` with OAuth client credentials; post creation requires both `youtube auth login` and `youtube auth login --credential-type browser_session` on the same profile.
